featureName: BUG-009-003: Topic mapping ignores ctx cancellation (stabilize R10)

executionHistory.evidence: Stochastic-quality-sweep sweep-2026-05-25-r10 round 10 random pick selected specs/009-bookmarks-connector with trigger `stabilize` mapped via workflows.yaml `triggerWorkflowModes.stabilize: stabilize-to-doc`. Executed parent-expanded child workflow mode because the active workflow runtime lacked recursive `runSubagent` for `bubbles.workflow`; expansion preserves the mapped child mode's phaseOrder and statusCeiling=done contract.

Created the BUG-009-003 folder with all 6 required artifacts before any code change: spec.md, design.md, scopes.md, report.md, uservalidation.md, state.json. Bug packet shape follows the precedent established by BUG-009-001 and BUG-009-002 under sibling sweep rounds. Pre-bootstrap planning artifacts in parent spec 009 were already certified `done`, so the bootstrap surface for this round is the bug folder only.

Pre-fix validation baseline captured: parent spec 009 status=done with all 2 scopes certified, BUG-009-001 and BUG-009-002 both closed `done`, last execution-history entry at 2026-05-23T00:30:00Z (R30 chaos). Bookmarks-package tests pass clean against pre-fix tree but provide ZERO coverage of the topic-mapping ctx-cancel path (TestSyncRespectsContextCancel only covered the pre-loop cancel boundary). Baseline established that the stabilize trigger had a legitimate gap to surface.

Parent-expanded stabilize-to-doc child workflow probe read internal/connector/bookmarks/connector.go end-to-end against the supervisor's cancellation contract (internal/connector/supervisor.go). One finding surfaced — F-STAB-R10-001 (MEDIUM: post-dedup topic-mapping loop ignored ctx.Err(), burning three doomed DB calls per remaining artifact on supervisor shutdown and emitting O(N) stale per-call slog.Warn instead of one structured cancel signal). Pre-existing TestSyncRespectsContextCancel only covered pre-loop cancel; mid-loop cancel had no coverage. R30/R24/R16 chaos rounds focused on file-scan and NormalizeURL, not on the topic-mapping loop.

Explicit no-op disposition: F-STAB-R10-001 is a pure in-process cancellation-hygiene fix on the bookmarks connector. No new ports, no new env vars, no docker-compose changes, no deploy compose changes, no host-bind changes, no migration scripts, no NATS subject changes, no Prometheus metric changes. Verified by `git diff --name-only HEAD` showing only internal/connector/bookmarks/connector.go and connector_test.go as production diff. Deploy-target adapters and bundle generation are unaffected; no devops phase work is in scope for this round.

Finding-owned planning: authored spec.md (classification + 1-finding reproduction + 7 acceptance criteria), design.md (Current Truth from codebase scan, root cause, extract-loop-and-guard-top-of-iteration fix approach with ordering rationale), scopes.md (single Scope 1 with 3 SCN-BK-FIX-003-NNN Gherkin scenarios + 6-row Test Plan including regression-E2E coverage row + 10 DoD bullets carrying scenario IDs and inline evidence).

Modified internal/connector/bookmarks/connector.go: extracted the post-dedup topic-mapping loop from Sync() into a new private method `func (c *BookmarksConnector) mapFolderTopics(ctx context.Context, artifacts []connector.RawArtifact) int`. Added ctx.Err() guard at the TOP of every iteration that emits one slog.Warn("bookmarks topic mapping cancelled mid-loop", "error", err, "processed", processed, "remaining", len(artifacts)-processed) and returns the processed count. Sync() now invokes c.mapFolderTopics(ctx, allArtifacts) in place of the inline loop; the c.mu.Lock(); c.lastSyncCount = ...; c.lastSyncTime = ...; c.mu.Unlock() block is unchanged. Signature of Sync() unchanged. Boundary: connector.go is the only production file modified. Diff stat: 99 lines (+67/-32) on connector.go; +109 lines on connector_test.go.

Added 3 adversarial regression tests to internal/connector/bookmarks/connector_test.go: TestStabR10_TopicMappingRespectsContextCancel (10-artifact slice; pre-cancelled ctx → processed==0; live ctx → processed==10), TestStabR10_TopicMappingCancelBeforeFirstIteration (1-artifact slice; pre-cancelled ctx → processed==0 — ordering invariant), TestStabR10_TopicMappingNilMapper (nil topicMapper via NewConnector → processed==0, no panic). `go test -count=1 -v -run TestStabR10 ./internal/connector/bookmarks/...` → 3/3 PASS, each with the expected single structured warn emission. Full package suite `go test ./internal/connector/bookmarks/... -count=1` → ok 0.158s. Red→green TDD evidence: tests authored against pre-fix tree FAIL with diagnostics `mapFolderTopics processed=10 on pre-cancelled ctx, want 0` and `ctx guard must run BEFORE the iteration body, not after: processed=1 on pre-cancelled ctx with 1 artifact, want 0`; after fix applied tests turn GREEN. Transcript recorded in report.md 'Adversarial Fidelity Transcript' section.

Consumer regression sweep `go test ./internal/connector/... ./internal/scheduler/...` PASSES all 21 connector packages plus the scheduler (one shared owner of bookmarks.Connector.Sync()). No regression in any pre-existing bookmarks-package test. Persistent scenario-specific regression coverage carried by T-BK-FIX-003-01/02/03 (3 tests live in connector_test.go and gate every future change to mapFolderTopics). Broader regression coverage carried by T-BK-FIX-003-04 (full bookmarks package) and T-BK-FIX-003-05 (consumer sweep across 21 connector packages + scheduler). Adversarial proof T-BK-FIX-003-06: toggling guard OFF turns T-BK-FIX-003-01/02 RED; restoring guard turns them GREEN — demonstrably catches reintroduction of the bug.

Refactor reduces Sync()'s in-method complexity: the 30-line inline topic-mapping block (nested if topicMapper != nil { for ... { folder extraction; MapFolder; if len(matches) > 0 { CreateTopicEdge; for ... { UpdateTopicMomentum } } } }) is extracted to a named private method `mapFolderTopics(ctx, artifacts) int` with a single responsibility. Sync()'s post-dedup region collapses from 30 lines of inline iteration to a single c.mapFolderTopics(ctx, allArtifacts) call site, reducing Sync()'s line count and nesting depth. The new method also embeds the nil-topicMapper guard at the entry, removing one outer `if c.topicMapper != nil` wrapper from Sync()'s body. Net result: better testability (the loop is now directly unit-testable without spinning up a full Sync()) and clearer ctx-cancel boundary.

Reviewed the diff for new attack surface: ZERO new input vectors. The new method takes the same `artifacts []connector.RawArtifact` and `ctx context.Context` already trusted at the Sync() boundary. The ctx.Err() guard HARDENS the connector against a doomed-context amplification path: pre-fix, supervisor shutdown could cause O(N) doomed DB queries (one MapFolder + one CreateTopicEdge + one UpdateTopicMomentum per remaining artifact) against the application's own database, each producing a stale slog.Warn that would flood logs and delay supervisor teardown. Post-fix, one structured warn replaces O(N) noise. No new secrets, no new SQL, no new network calls, no new file IO, no new deserialization. No new privileged paths. Auth/authorization paths unchanged.

The stabilize trigger of this round IS the chaos probe — it injected the context-cancellation fault into the topic-mapping path and surfaced the gap. Deterministic regression coverage of the chaos input is carried by the 3 R10 tests, which exercise: (a) pre-cancelled ctx with N=10 artifacts (mid-loop cancel) — SCN-BK-FIX-003-001, (b) pre-cancelled ctx with N=1 artifact (cancel-before-first-iteration; proves guard ordering) — SCN-BK-FIX-003-002, (c) live ctx — baseline non-cancel path executes all 10 artifacts. No additional random fault-injection is in scope for this round; the parent sweep ledger pin enforces persistent chaos coverage of the cancel signal across all future sweep rounds via the persistent regression tests.

`go vet ./internal/connector/bookmarks/...` exits 0 with no output. `gofmt -l internal/connector/bookmarks/connector.go internal/connector/bookmarks/connector_test.go` exits 0 with no output. Boundary verified: `git diff --name-only HEAD -- internal/connector/bookmarks/` returns connector.go and connector_test.go only. Unrelated working-tree files (deploy/CI, deploy-target-status, framework-stats) are NOT in this round's commit set — parent sweep finalize reconciles those separately. Pre-existing FAKE_INTEGRATION violations flagged by implementation-reality-scan on `return nil` and `return nil, fmt.Errorf(...)` patterns are documented in report.md 'Guard Disposition' — they are heuristic false positives on legitimate Go error-return idioms, all PRE-EXISTING (parent spec 009 `done` and BUG-009-002 `done` both currently flag the same lines), and NONE are introduced by this round's diff.

Appended Stabilize R10 execution-history entry to specs/009-bookmarks-connector/state.json; appended 'Stabilize R10 — Topic-mapping cancel hardening' section to specs/009-bookmarks-connector/report.md naming this bug and F-STAB-R10-001 with Fixed status. scenario-manifest.json authored next to spec/design/scopes covering SCN-BK-FIX-003-001/002/003 with linkedTests pointing at the three TestStabR10_* functions in connector_test.go. Code Diff Evidence section added to report.md with the diff stat and a representative excerpt of the new mapFolderTopics method.

Path-limited stage: connector.go + connector_test.go + parent state.json/report.md + this bug folder. Single fix-bundle commit with conventional message `bubbles(009/sweep-r10-stabilize-pass): topic mapping respects ctx cancel`. Sweep ledger pin commit follows with the post-commit SHA recorded in `.specify/memory/sweep-2026-05-25-r10.json::rounds[10]`. Push to origin/main runs under the standard SSH_AUTH_SOCK arming; the pre-push hook validates the working tree against the project's full pre-push contract (NEVER --no-verify on .go diffs).

scopeProgress.name: Guard the post-dedup topic-mapping loop against ctx cancellation
